Document:
# Rule A3: Current-rounding is forbidden
Current-rounding (sharing info about the current round) is inherently disruptive to a roleplay environment. - Sharing information your character would not normally have in a round whether in LOOC/OOC chats or on another platform; both are explicitly forbidden. - Streaming your game live to others is allowed, but anyone caught in that same round and watching your stream will be in trouble for it. - It goes without saying that encouraging this behavior will also get you, the streamer, in trouble.
## Examples: Allowed: - Talking on Discord about the manifest that round (this information is already available to anyone in the lobby). - Streaming on Discord or Twitch without interacting with your viewers in-game.
Prohibited: - You just rolled an antagonist role or got converted into one and used LOOC chat to mention it. - [color=#ff0000]This still applies if you do this in front of other people who know about it in-character, as other people can still hear your message through walls. There is zero tolerance for this and you will be punished for it.[/color] - You told someone else what the current gamemode is, somebody's antagonist status, what you have on you or actions you've done this shift when they wouldn't normally have access to that information. - After taking a ghost role or being revived you gave others information you obtained while observing.
[color=#ff0000]This is a high severity rule to break and our staff will have little tolerance for it. Don't test your luck.[/color] [color=#ff0000]If you have any questions or concerns, please ask an admin via the AHelp (F1) chat.[/color]
